name: CEIT 325 Classroom
subtitle: Teaching floor and student access layer
summary: The main instructional space where students meet the ecosystem, use classroom PCs as access terminals, and connect coursework to live SOTE infrastructure.
sees: Rows of student workstations, dual-NIC lab access paths, briefing screens, and the first visible entry point into Mission Control.
mission: Students begin with safe observation, lab citizenship, documentation, and First Signal orientation before operating deeper systems.
student_tasks: Explain how classroom PCs connect to the lab
system_notes: 24 student workstations with lab access path
Works as the public and teaching entry point for the ecosystem
Best zone for tours, open houses, and first-day orientation

name: Mission Control
subtitle: Network operations and command center
summary: Mission Control is the operator floor for network validation, console access, status checks, troubleshooting, ticket review, and student handoffs.
sees: Rack 1 switching, console workflows, monitoring views, status boards, and a command-center style operating rhythm.
mission: Students learn to observe the environment, connect safely, diagnose issues, document evidence, and escalate with clear notes.
system_notes: Rack 1 - Mission Control access switching
Mission Control Console Array concept
Command Wall and operations display path

name: Rocket's Forge
subtitle: Teaching datacenter and virtualization core
summary: Rocket's Forge hosts the SOTE services that make the ecosystem real: Proxmox, storage, templates, Windows and Linux teaching platforms, documentation, and management services.
sees: Servers, aggregation switching, storage, VM templates, Proxmox hosts, and the build environment for student-operated infrastructure.
mission: Students build services, provision VMs, validate templates, manage storage paths, and learn that infrastructure depends on repeatable documentation.
student_tasks: Stage an ISO or VM template
system_notes: Rack 2 - Rocket's Forge
R2-PVE01/02/03 Proxmox host plan
Shared storage, image repository, and backup path

name: Western Pennsylvania Cyber Defense Command
subtitle: Cyber range and defense operations
summary: The Western Pennsylvania Cyber Defense Command is the cyber range and scenario space where students practice safe defense, investigation, recovery, and incident communication.
sees: Cyber range systems, blue-team scenarios, controlled vulnerable machines, security monitoring concepts, and Sir Kingston watchlist language.
mission: Students learn to defend systems, investigate signals, document findings, and separate approved lab work from production environments.
student_tasks: Explain the boundary between lab and production
system_notes: Rack 3 - Western Pennsylvania Cyber Defense Command
HP c3000 and BL460c Gen9 foundation
R930 storage and image repository expansion path

name: Patch Bay Alpha
subtitle: Physical-layer staging and security edge bay
summary: Patch Bay Alpha turns the physical layer into a student learning space for cabling, tracing, patching, documentation, router staging, and firewall staging.
sees: Patch rack, blue cabling, orange wall treatment, cable tray, security-edge staging equipment, and the approved slogan: CONNECT. PATCH. VERIFY.
mission: Students trace the path before they change the path. Every cable should have a purpose, a label, and a ticket or worksheet note.
student_tasks: Label both ends of a cable
Document a router or firewall staging port
Verify link lights and port mapping
system_notes: Patch Bay Alpha security-edge staging bay
Fortinet, Palo Alto, and Cisco 2900 staging concept
Companion zone to Rack 2/Rack 3 security boundary work

name: Rocket's Engineering Bay
subtitle: R&D, staging, and hardware preparation
summary: Rocket's Engineering Bay is the workbench environment for hardware staging, server preparation, parts validation, and engineering experiments.
sees: Bench space, spare servers, rails, storage components, parts staging, hardware inspection, and student engineering notes.
mission: Students learn that professional infrastructure work begins before equipment is racked: inspect, stage, label, test, and document.
student_tasks: Move parts into the correct locker or ticket
system_notes: Supports R730/R820/R930 staging
Connects to Rocket's Parts Locker and Quartermaster Bay concept
Good zone for hardware inventory and pre-build tickets

name: Ace's Support Command
subtitle: Student help desk and support workflow
summary: Ace's Support Command is the support desk identity for SOTE: ticket intake, simulated user support, account workflows, and student communication practice.
sees: ACE Service Desk workflow, ticket queue concepts, frontline support language, and student-facing help desk identity.
mission: Students practice professional support: clarify the problem, document the request, perform approved work, verify the fix, and close the loop.
student_tasks: Connect support action to a badge or course outcome
system_notes: ACE Service Desk / GLPI proposal path
R2-HDSK01 target service identity
Help Desk Hero badge alignment

name: Command Briefing Room
subtitle: Planning, debriefs, and tour storytelling
summary: The Command Briefing Room supports planning, tours, donor conversations, student debriefs, publication planning, and instructor coordination.
sees: Briefing table, visitor conversations, project reviews, mission notes, and space for students to explain what they built.
mission: Students learn to communicate technical work clearly, connect tasks to outcomes, and brief stakeholders with confidence.
student_tasks: Capture next actions from a debrief
system_notes: Best location for tours and stakeholder briefings
Connects website, one-sheet, status board, and publication pipeline
Supports Foundation and donor engagement storytelling

name: Rocket's Launch Pad
subtitle: Wiring lab and hands-on skills validation
summary: Rocket's Launch Pad is the wiring and skills validation space for cabling, punchdown practice, patch panels, simulated offices, and launch missions.
sees: Structured cabling practice, cable testing, patch panels, wiring diagrams, and skills checkoff activities.
mission: Students build confidence by turning physical skills into visible evidence: terminate, test, label, and document.
student_tasks: Earn Cable Runner or related mission badge
system_notes: Supports cabling labs consumables proposal
Connects to student console kits and wiring missions
Good public demo zone for hands-on skills
